news.headline: Delcath Systems, Inc. Announces Inducement Grants Under Nasdaq Listing Rule 5635(c)(4)
news.summary: Delcath granted 17,000 restricted stock units, effective July 1, 2026, as employment inducements under its 2023 Inducement Plan to four new employees who joined in May and June 2026. The routine Nasdaq-required disclosure points to continued headcount additions as the company scales its commercial and clinical operations.

news.headline: BTIG Raises Delcath Systems Price Target to $17 from $16, Maintains Buy
news.summary: BTIG analyst Marie Thibault raised her price target on Delcath Systems to $17 from $16 while reiterating a Buy rating. The modest increase reflects continued confidence in HEPZATO KIT's commercial trajectory following the company's Q1 2026 print and reiterated full-year revenue guidance.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces First Patient Dosed in Phase 2 Clinical Trial of HEPZATO in Liver-Dominant Metastatic Breast Cancer
news.summary: Delcath dosed the first patient, at the European Institute of Oncology in Milan, in its global randomized Phase 2 DELUMA trial testing HEPZATO plus standard of care versus standard of care alone in liver-dominant HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer. The roughly 90-patient trial is planned to run at more than 20 sites across the US and Europe.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Two ASCO 2026 Investigator Initiated Trials-in-Progress Abstracts
news.summary: Two investigator-initiated trial-in-progress abstracts combining Delcath's percutaneous hepatic perfusion (PHP) with newer systemic therapies -- sequential PHP followed by tebentafusp in metastatic uveal melanoma, and PHP plus nivolumab/relatlimab in liver-metastatic melanoma -- were accepted as posters at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting in Chicago. The studies aim to show how liver-directed PHP can be sequenced or combined with modern immunotherapy regimens.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Reports First Quarter 2026 Results and Business Highlights
news.summary: Q1 2026 revenue rose to $25.0 million from $19.8 million a year earlier, with HEPZATO KIT contributing $23.3 million on roughly 36% volume growth, and the company reiterated full-year 2026 revenue guidance of at least $100 million. Delcath ended the quarter with $89.3 million in cash and a narrow $1.1 million net loss.

news.headline: Delcath Systems Announces Presentation of New Data on Percutaneous Hepatic Perfusion with Melphalan in Liver-Dominant Metastatic Breast Cancer at ESMO Breast Cancer 2026
news.summary: Independent European investigators presented a retrospective analysis of 15 liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer patients treated with Delcath's CHEMOSAT (M-PHP) at the ESMO Breast Cancer 2026 congress in Berlin, reporting hepatic partial responses in 9 of 15 patients. The data helped build the rationale behind the newly launched Phase 2 DELUMA breast cancer trial.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings report
catalysts.detail: Analyst earnings calendars (Zacks, Nasdaq) point to roughly August 5, 2026, consistent with the prior two years' early-August reporting dates, but Delcath had not formally announced a Q2 2026 call date as of July 23, 2026. Management is expected to update HEPZATO volume growth, site-activation pace, and progress toward the $100M+ FY2026 revenue guidance.

catalysts.label: Full-year 2026 revenue guidance of at least $100 million
catalysts.detail: Reiterated on the Q1 2026 call (May 7, 2026), implying continued double-digit growth in HEPZATO KIT volume over 2025's $85.2 million in revenue; delivery against this target is the key near-term proof point ahead of Q2 results.

catalysts.label: HEPZATO active treatment center expansion to ~40 sites
catalysts.detail: Management guided to roughly 37 active US centers by year-end 2026 and 40 by Q1 2027, a key driver of continued HEPZATO volume growth; four new centers were activated in Q1 2026 alone.

catalysts.label: Phase 2 DELUMA trial in liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer
catalysts.detail: First patient was dosed July 9, 2026 in the ~90-patient randomized trial of HEPZATO plus standard of care vs. standard of care alone; enrollment across 20+ US/European sites is the near-term milestone, with efficacy data still to come.

catalysts.label: Phase 2 trial in liver-dominant metastatic colorectal cancer
catalysts.detail: Randomized ~90-patient study of HEPZATO plus trifluridine-tipiracil/bevacizumab vs. that regimen alone in third-line colorectal cancer patients, with site activation ongoing across a planned ~26 centers. Primary endpoint (hepatic progression-free survival) is anticipated around mid-2028, with overall survival data expected in late 2028.

pulse: News flow on Delcath has been light but steadily positive over the past 45 days: the July 9 dosing of the first patient in the new Phase 2 DELUMA trial (HEPZATO in liver-dominant metastatic breast cancer) extends the pipeline beyond uveal melanoma and colorectal cancer, while BTIG nudged its price target up to $17 on July 13 and the company filed a routine Nasdaq inducement-grant disclosure on July 16 tied to continued hiring. Underlying the quiet news cycle is a solid commercial base -- Q1 2026 revenue of $25.0 million on ~36% HEPZATO volume growth, with full-year guidance of at least $100 million reiterated -- and growing clinical/guideline validation (ASCO 2026 abstracts, ESMO Breast Cancer data). The next real catalyst is Q2 2026 earnings, expected around early August 2026 but not yet formally scheduled as of July 23, 2026.
